notes: Free HTML-based seller profile page. Operational by 1998. Allowed sellers to personalize presence and build trust.
Launched March 2025. Rule-based automation for Promoted Listings with auto ad rate adjustment.
Payment solution launched as beta Q4 1999. Phased out January 2003 after PayPal acquisition.
135-year-old San Francisco art/antiques auction house acquired May 1999 for ~$260M. Sold to Bonhams 2002.
Commercial agreement + investment announced Nov 2023. 100M+ cards under management. Not acquisition, but partnership.

canonical_name: Circular Fashion Innovator of the Year
notes: Special $300K prize within Circular Fashion Fund. New for 2025 cohort.
Acquired December 2010. Talent acquisition for mobile strategy. Not in baseline.
First third-party licensing partner, November 1996. Used SmartMarket Technology for travel auctions.
Launched February 26, 1996 by Pierre Omidyar. Original feedback/rating system. Foundation of eBay's trust infrastructure.
Android-specific variant of Image Search launched Fall 2017. Campaign name also appears in 2024 Goldin integration. May be umbrella campaign vs product feature.
Announced March 28, 2011. Closed June 20, 2011. Rebranded to eBay Enterprise June 20, 2013. Listed in baseline as [Legacy].
Active by 2013-2014 based on sources. Baseline lists as [Legacy] coexisting with eBay International Shipping. Major driver of eBay's export business.
NFT marketplace acquired June 2022. Baseline mentions 'NFT Sales' but not KnownOrigin as a product name. Founded 2018 in Manchester, UK.
Automobile auctioneer acquired 1999 for $165M stock. Launched automotive category, precursor to eBay Motors. Repurchased by Dean Kruse 2002.
UK-specific fashion campaign from 2012. Part of eBay's fashion vertical initiatives. Not found in baseline campaigns section.
Initial investment March 2010, full acquisition announced June 6, 2011, completed August 2011. Core component of X.commerce platform. Not in baseline.
Extension of Magical Listing Tool for batch processing. Baseline only mentions 'Magical Listing' singular.
Retired 2015, replaced by Donation Account within eBay Giving Works/eBay for Charity.
Completed 2015. Major corporate event. Not listed in baseline as it's corporate structure rather than product/program naming.
Early fraud and dispute reporting system, contactable via SafeHarbor@eBay.com. Precursor to modern Trust & Safety programs.
Debuted September 2015, full US rollout August 2016. Listed in baseline as [Product] under Seller Tools & Hub umbrella.
Part of GSI Commerce acquisition but mostly divested. eBay had minority ownership 2011-2013. Not a core eBay product but associated during this period.
eBay's auction platform technology licensed to Electronic Travel Auction (November 1996). First third-party licensing deal. May be internal tech name.
Acquired Oct 2022 for ~$295M. Marketplace for collectible card games, operates autonomously.
Authentication service operated by TCGplayer.
The marketplace platform within TCGplayer. May be considered same as parent TCGplayer entry.

canonical_name: Techstars Future of Ecommerce powered by eBay
notes: Accelerator program launched Sept 2023 with Techstars.
New creative platform/brand strategy launched with eBay Evo.
Acquired Oct 2025. Norway-based C2C social marketplace for Gen Z/Millennial secondhand. eBay Ventures invested 2022.
Acquired July 2015. Bolt-on acquisition for eBay Valet fashion expansion. Not mentioned in baseline.
Trading site acquired in 1998.
Announced October 2011 at Innovate conference. Unified eBay/PayPal/Magento developer ecosystem. Platform active 2011-2013 era. Not in baseline.
Launched 2011 with CFDA partnership. Major fashion authenticity initiative during fashion vertical push. Not in baseline campaigns.
Part of X.commerce 2011. Mobile payments via carrier billing. Unclear if acquisition or partnership. Not in baseline.
Referenced in eBay Inc. stories. Major app version from mobile-first era. Not explicitly in baseline.
Public launch August 2010 after year-long pilot. Described as 'first-ever free rewards program'. Listed in baseline as [Legacy] under Loyalty & Membership umbrella.
Vertical mobile app retired 2015. Migrated to flagship app.
Held 2011 (at minimum). Announced X.commerce. Not listed in baseline Events section (which lists eBay Open but not Innovate).
Acquisition announced December 2, 2010. Founded 2007, raised only $5M in VC. Strategic play for $917B online-to-offline market. Integrated into RedLaser by December 2010.
Launched February 16, 2010. Early mover in mobile commerce alongside iOS app. Baseline lists 'eBay Motors App' but not general Android/iOS apps from this era.
Launched September 2013. Regional fashion app for Latin America. Not found in baseline regional products.
Launched 2012, retired 2015. Pilots in SF, NYC, San Jose. Listed in baseline as [Legacy] under Marketplace Variants.
Comprehensive brand guide with 280+ pages, 2700+ images/videos. Part of eBay Evo initiative.
Acquisition announced June 23, 2010. App had 2M+ downloads pre-acquisition. Reached 9M downloads by March 2011. Part of eBay's mobile commerce strategy 2010-2015.
Launched June 17, 2014. Closed 2018. Listed in baseline as [Legacy] under Premium Services.
